title: verbascum cheiranthifolium var. asperulum
intro: Verbascum cheiranthifolium var. asperulum is a variety of the Verbascum cheiranthifolium plant, commonly known as the mullein or great mullein. It is a biennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its large, hairy leaves and tall spikes of yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions and wounds. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves, and is often used to soothe coughs, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
